```
public long convertLocalToUTC (long instantLocal, boolean strict) {
    int offsetLocal = getOffset(instantLocal);
    int offset = getOffset(instantLocal - offsetLocal);
    if (offsetLocal != offset) {
        if (strict || offsetLocal < 0) {
            long nextLocal = nextTransition(instantLocal - offsetLocal);
            if (nextLocal == (instantLocal - offsetLocal)) {

            }
        }

}
```

In the original code, if the offset changes when moving from instantLocal to (instantLocal - offsetLocal), it proceeds to check whether strict mode or if the offset is negative, then finds the next transition and so on.

By changing the condition to always be true, we're effectively removing the check for whether the offset has changed. This might lead to incorrect behavior in cases where the offset hasn't actually changed but the code still attempts to adjust it.
